core_principle: Compile when you can measure. The optimizer maximizes your metric — garbage metric in, garbage prompt out.

gates.name: MEASURE
gates.predicate: metric exists AND human-validated >=20 spot-checks (>=30 open-ended), >=80% agreement, NOT a lone holistic LLM-judge

gates.name: DATA
gates.predicate: labeled example count >= floor of the chosen optimizer
gates.on_fail: drop to a lower-floor optimizer, collect data, or STOP (LabeledFewShot as floor)

operations.name: ReadinessGate
operations.trigger: Anyone about to call an optimizer's compile()
operations.action: Run Gate 1 (metric exists + validated >=20) then Gate 2 (examples >= optimizer floor). Any failure -> STOP.
operations.output: Decision token: compile-ready | not-ready + failing gate

operations.name: MetricExistsCheck
operations.action: Confirm metric(example, pred, trace=None) -> bool|float exists. If absent -> route to [[metric-design]], gate stays closed.

operations.name: MetricValidatedCheck
operations.action: Require >=20 (open-ended >=30) human spot-checks at >=80% agreement + calibration receipt. If lone holistic LLM-judge, require decomposition first.

operations.name: ExampleFloorCheck
operations.action: Count labeled examples; compare to floor of intended optimizer (LabeledFewShot any; GEPA ~10+feedback; Bootstrap ~30-50; MIPROv2 200+).

operations.name: OptimizerByDataScale
operations.action: Pick from optimizer-by-data-scale table by example count + feedback signal. Prefer GEPA when textual feedback exists (inverts data-scale floor).

operations.name: CostBudgetGuard
operations.action: Estimate num_trials x |trainset| x calls; cap spend; set a cheap optimizer LM (gpt-4o-mini); enable track_usage=True.

operations.name: CheapProbeFirst
operations.action: Run auto="light" (or smallest config) first. Never start at heavy.

operations.name: EscalateOrReturn
operations.action: <2% lift -> return to program/metric design (do NOT escalate). 2-10% -> medium. heavy only if data >=300 + held-out test set. Confirm gain on held-out test.

optimizer_by_data_scale.note: No search; weakest. Usually STOP and collect data.
Inverts the data-scale rule; sample-efficient reflection on text feedback.
Joint instruction+demo Bayesian search; 200 documented floor.

cost_budget.use_when: only if data >=300 + held-out test + budget

dilemma_cases.title: Optimizer cost vs gain: is compiling even worth it?
dilemma_cases.scenario: 3-stage RAG at 72% dev after manual tuning; MIPROv2 auto=heavy would cost ~$40/4h; 250 examples; task LM gpt-4o.
dilemma_cases.resolution: Gate 1 (was metric ever driving design?) -> probe auto=light (~$2) never heavy -> <2% lift means fix program/metric not add compute; 2-10% escalate to medium; use gpt-4o-mini optimizer LM.
dilemma_cases.lesson: Never open at heavy. Probe light with a cheap optimizer LM; treat <2% as a signal to fix the program, not to add compute.

dilemma_cases.title: Only 12 examples: GEPA inverts the data-scale assumption
dilemma_cases.scenario: Code-fix agent with 12 labeled examples but rich textual feedback (test diffs, schema violations, linter errors).
dilemma_cases.resolution: Gate 1 passes (test is ground truth). Do NOT apply scalar data ladder: textual feedback makes GEPA legal at ~10 examples. Pick GEPA returning dspy.Prediction(score, feedback); probe cheap; confirm on held-out.
dilemma_cases.lesson: The example floor is per-optimizer. If you can express why an output failed as text, GEPA inverts the data-scale assumption (~10 examples suffice). Don't reflexively gate out small feedback-rich datasets.
dilemma_cases.source: dspy-sop optimizer table + Case C step 5; arxiv.org/abs/2507.19457

anti_patterns.name: Compiling against an un-validated single LLM-judge
Compiling on <10 examples (scalar feedback)
anti_patterns.fix: Collect data / LabeledFewShot floor / GEPA if textual feedback (OP-4)

anti_patterns.name: Using the expensive task LM as the optimizer LM
Applying the scalar data-floor to a feedback-rich task
anti_patterns.fix: Check for textual feedback before counting examples (OP-5)

anti_patterns.name: Reporting gain on the val set used in optimization
Compiling while the Signature/I-O contract still churns

cross_framework.dspy_optimizers: prompt instructions+demos (optionally weights via BootstrapFinetune); metric REQUIRED; floors 10/30/200; both gates apply directly
cross_framework.manual_few_shot: hand-edited prompt string; implicit/eyeballed metric (the failure mode); Gate 1 is exactly what's missing
cross_framework.openai_finetuning: weights path; needs trustworthy held-out eval set (~50-100+ examples practical min); gate logic transfers, operators differ
